prompt: Build a brief on local AI deepfake election misinformation.

sampleOutput.summary: Deepfake misuse is moving from national politics into local races where verification capacity is weaker.

angles.title: Election integrity operations
angles.why: Local election offices lack specialized forensic resources.

angles.title: Platform policy enforcement
angles.why: Detection and takedown times vary widely by platform.

angles.title: Voter trust and literacy
angles.why: Confusion can suppress turnout and trust in outcomes.

background.fact: Multiple jurisdictions have issued guidance on manipulated media ahead of elections.
Researchers report lower confidence in identifying AI-generated content without context.
Several provinces are discussing legal remedies for synthetic media harms.

relatedCoverage.headline: Cities prepare for AI misinformation
relatedCoverage.takeaway: Municipal teams are piloting rapid verification workflows.

suggestedVoices.name: Alex Example
suggestedVoices.why: Can explain legal guardrails and enforcement gaps.

interviewQuestions: What is the fastest practical verification workflow for local newsrooms?
Which deepfake signals are most reliable for producers?
How should local election officials communicate uncertainty to voters?
What policy changes would reduce harm before the next election cycle?
Where do current platform moderation rules fail local contexts?
watchouts: Do not overstate deepfake prevalence without local incident data.
